scenes.speech: Let's automate repetitive tasks with the magic of loops!
scenes.title: Loops

scenes.speech: A loop that repeats a set number of times!
scenes.title: Basic for Loop
scenes.content: for i in range(5): print(i) outputs 0,1,2,3,4. Also works with lists and strings!
scenes.highlight: for i in range(5): → repeats 0 to 4

scenes.speech: Repeats while a condition is True!
scenes.title: while Loop
scenes.content: while count < 10: count += 1 repeats until the condition is False. Watch out for infinite loops!

scenes.title: break Statement
scenes.content: break forcefully stops the loop. Use it when you've found the answer and don't need to continue!

scenes.title: continue Statement
scenes.content: continue skips the current iteration and moves to the next. Use to process only even numbers, etc.!

scenes.speech: Process all elements of a list in order!
scenes.title: Looping Over Lists
scenes.content: for item in ['apple','orange','banana']: print(item) processes every item in the list!
scenes.highlight: for item in list: → process all elements

scenes.speech: Get index and value at the same time!
scenes.title: Using enumerate()
scenes.content: for i, v in enumerate(['a','b','c']): gets i=0,1,2 and v='a','b','c' simultaneously!
scenes.highlight: enumerate() → get number + value at once

scenes.title: List Comprehension
scenes.content: [x*2 for x in range(5)] creates [0,2,4,6,8]. Code becomes short and clean!
scenes.highlight: [expr for var in list] → write it short!

scenes.title: Nested Loops
scenes.content: Use nested loops for multiplication tables and 2D array processing. Watch out for computation time!
scenes.highlight: for i: → for j: → Multiplication table done

scenes.speech: You can now use loops like a pro!
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
